success_surface: Sheet-level string extraction and column separation while preserving file integrity
effective_behaviors: Followed answer_position constraints precisely to limit processing scope
Used openpyxl for Excel-native file operations requiring structure preservation
Implemented null/empty value checks before string manipulation
Applied flexible pattern matching with fallback logic for variable data formats
Saved modified content to separate output path to maintain source file integrity
Added post-execution verification to confirm changes were applied correctly
Stripped whitespace from extracted substrings for clean data presentation
evidence: Processed only rows 1-12 on 'Before' sheet per answer_position specification
Used wb.cell() method for precise cell addressing within the target range
Checked 'if value is not None and str(value).strip()' before processing each cell
Attempted specific pattern match (event_type=) first, then fell back to alphabetic character search
Called os.makedirs() to ensure output directory exists before saving
Printed verification summary showing row-by-row column A and B values after modification
Preserved header row while processing data rows within the specified range

memory_items.title: Range Constraint Enforcement
memory_items.lesson: Always parse and strictly follow the answer_position or instruction-specified cell ranges to avoid over-processing or under-processing.
memory_items.generalization: When given explicit cell ranges or worksheet specifications, programmatically determine row/column boundaries and iterate only within those bounds.

memory_items.title: Null-Safe String Processing
memory_items.lesson: Check for None/null values and empty strings before applying any string transformations to prevent runtime errors.
memory_items.generalization: Implement defensive programming patterns: validate cell content exists and contains meaningful data before transformation logic executes.

memory_items.title: Flexible Data Parsing Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Try primary pattern detection first, then implement fallback logic for edge cases where expected patterns are absent.
memory_items.generalization: Design string manipulation with tiered approach: prioritize specific delimiters/patterns, fall back to generic rules like finding first alphabetic character.

memory_items.title: Verification-First Execution Flow
memory_items.lesson: After completing modifications, output verification data to confirm changes matched expectations before finalizing.
memory_items.generalization: Include a validation phase that logs sample results or summaries to enable quick debugging and quality assurance of the transformation.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Range Constraint Enforcement**: Always parse and strictly follow the answer_position or instruction-specified cell ranges to avoid over-processing or under-processing. Programmatically determine row/column boundaries and iterate only within those bounds.
- **Null-Safe String Processing**: Check for None/null values and empty strings before applying any string transformations to prevent runtime errors. Implement defensive programming patterns: validate cell content exists and contains meaningful data before transformation logic executes.
- **Flexible Data Parsing Strategy**: Try primary pattern detection first, then implement fallback logic for edge cases where expected patterns are absent. Design string manipulation with tiered approach: prioritize specific delimiters/patterns, fall back to generic rules.
- **Verification-First Execution Flow**: After completing modifications, output verification data to confirm changes matched expectations before finalizing. Include a validation phase that logs sample results or summaries to enable quick debugging and quality assurance.
